title: acetosa bucephalophora
intro: Acetosa bucephalophora, also known as Oxalis tuberosa, is a perennial plant native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Acetosa bucephalophora include the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as the management of diabetes and other metabolic disorders. Acetosa bucephalophora can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make a sweet, starchy root, known as oca, which is rich in fiber and minerals.
